name: Fondue Bread with Sliced Apples and Chutney
recipeIngredient: 1 Pound frozen white bread dough, thawed according to
recipeInstructions:
1. Adjust oven rack to middle position. Heat oven to 325°F. Grease a 9-inch springform pan with butter or margarine.
2. Reserve 1 egg white for later use. Beat the yolks of 2 eggs and 1 egg white in a medium bowl. Add cheese and rosemary; stir to combine and set aside.
3. Roll the thawed dough on a lightly floured work surface into a rectangle about 6 inches by 20 inches. Place the cheese mixture lengthwise on the center of the dough. Fold the dough over the filling and pinch to seal the seam. Place the roll seam-side down around the outside of the prepared springform pan to make a ring; pinch the ends to seal. Brush with sliced almonds.
4. Bake for 1 hour. Release the pan hinge and remove the bread immediately, allowing it to rest on a wire rack for 15 minutes before serving. Slice apples and place them in a bowl of cold water mixed with 1 tablespoon lemon juice.
5. Place the cheese-filled bread in the center of a large round platter. Pat the apples dry and arrange overlapping in small groupings around the bread. Garnish with sprigs of fresh rosemary, if desired. Serve with chutney, if desired. Cut the bread into wedges.
Advance preparation: Assemble and freeze, unbaked. Thaw 6 hours in the refrigerator. Increase baking time by 5 minutes.
Note: Cheese will shred more easily in the food processor if it is very cold. Place it in the freezer for 10 minutes before grating.
